rulings.Case Title: MARK BONIN et al VS. BURGER BAR SAN FRANCISCO LLC., A LIMITED LIABILITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatoriesn - General, Form Interrogaories - Employment Law, Request For Production Of Documents, And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 20, 2013, Line 1, PLAINTIFFS MARK BONIN, AMANDA ROSE, KENNETH KOLICH, BROOKE MAGUIRE, TARA GALLEGOS' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatoriesn - General, Form Interrogaories - Employment Law, Request For Production Of Documents, And Request For Sanctions Granted as to all requests except Requests for Production 10 and 20 and Form Interrogatory 15.1. Plaintiffs are entitled to identity and contact information and the other information sought as relevant to the questions of class composition and identification of witnesses. Named Plaintiffs are not entitled to paycheck deductions and payroll tax records for putative class members at this time, but they are entitled to their own records called for by RFPs 10 and 20, so those requests must be responded to as to the named plaintiffs only. Plaintiffs did not adequately raise Form Interrogatory 15.1 during the meet and confer process, so the motion is denied as to that request. Defendant shall serve supplemental responses within 20 days. Monetary sanctions of $1125 are awarded to Plaintiff and against Defendants and their counsel to be paid no later than March 1, 2013. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery motion. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: MARK BONIN et al VS. BURGER BAR SAN FRANCISCO LLC., A LIMITED LIABILITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Moton To Deny Class Certification
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 20, 2013, Line 1, DEFENDANTS BURGER BAR SAN FRANCISCO LLC., A LIMITED LIABILITY, HUBERT KELLER AND JAMES BEAUPARLANT'S Motion To Deny Class Certification Denied without prejudice to re-filing if Plaintiffs have not filed a motion for class certification by July 20, 2013. Although Defendants have raised significant issues concerning the class definition as pleaded, one of the named plaintiffs, and one of class counsel, they have not shown that no class can be certified. The Court has various tools to deal with those issues, if necessary. When and if Plaintiffs file a motion for class certification, they should address the concerns raised in this motion. The objection to the Vereschagin Declaration filed January 4, 2013 is sustained. The declaration was untimely filed without permission. The Court also did not consider the unauthorized and untimely Sur-Reply Declaration. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL CONEFF VS. A & B PAINTING INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 20, 2013, Line 7, DEFENDANT A & B PAINTING INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Denied. Defendant failed to shift the burden to show that Plaintiff did not have a disability when he applied for work with Defendant in October 2010. Even if Defendant's evidence was sufficient to shift the burden, Plaintiff has raised a triable issue regarding whether Defendants discriminated on the basis of a perceived disability and whether Plaintiff was disabled. (See Plaintiff's Additional Material Facts Nos. 116-118 and 144-145.) Although Plaintiff cannot establish his diagnosis through his own testimony (and Defendants' objections are sustained to that extent), his testimony is admissible to establish his understanding of his symptoms and the effects of his condition, such as his need to take breaks for fresh air (and the objections are overruled to that extent). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and is in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: CALHOUN BROTHERS GRADING & PAVING, INC., A VS. CAL-PACIFIC CONSTRUCTION, INC., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Cal-Pacific Construction, Inc.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 20, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 4, Defendant Cal-Pacific Construction, Inc.'s Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order; Request For Judicial Notice Pro Tem Judge Elizabeth Riles, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion denied as moot. Plaintiff must file a motion to compel further responses should it wish further responses to the discovery propounded. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to eriles@brhrlaw.comwith a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Request for Admissions are deemed admitted by Defendant. Further, Defendant Max Audience and Defendant's Counsel, Paul Jacobson, are jointly ordered to pay sanctions of $635.00 to Plaintiff. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to eriles@brhrlaw.comwith a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: WYDE VOICE LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY et al VS. GLOBAL IP SOLUTIONS INC., et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Global Ip Solutions Inc. & Google Inc.'S Motion To Stay
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 20, 2013, Line 27, Defendants Global Ip Solutions Inc. & Google Inc.'S Motion To Stay The motion is denied. The Court chooses not to exercise its discretion to stay a portion of this case at this stage given that the call blocking issue is but one aspect of the alleged anti-competitive conduct and that Defendant has argued that analysis of the Order's language can determine its applicability to the case, an exercise with which state courts are familiar. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN PAGE VS. MICHAEL WORNICK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 20, 2013, Line 24, DEFENDANT COLONY SPECIALTY INSURANCE COMPANY, ARGONAUT INSURANCE COMPANY, DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT The demurrer is overruled as to Colony Specialty Insurance Company ("CSIC"). The complaint pleads that CSIC is an insuring entity, and it cannot be determined from the face of the complaint or its exhibits that CSIC, which is included on the signature page of the policy, did not insure Plaintiff. The demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to Argonaut, because its inclusion is inconsistent with the face of Exhibit 2 to the Complaint and is unsupported by any other charging allegations that could support its obligation under the contract. The objection to the Doherty Declaration is sustained. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/PJB)
